description: People form a community, ice forms on water, or separate parts acquire an organised structure. 'norelu' centres the coming about of that definite arrangement. 'kire' (shape) concerns a thing's configuration or drawn form, while 'norelu' can organise physical or abstract parts into a whole.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and let air pass through the nose into middle-height back rounded 'o'. Tap the tongue tip against the alveolar ridge before stressed middle-height front 'e', then return the tip to the ridge for 'l' and finish with high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The tongue moves from dental nasal to tap to lateral while the voice continues. Those linked changes give 'norelu' a gathered, settling motion.
usage_notes: The organiser is the subject and the emerging structure is the direct object. Passive 'se' centres a structure whose organiser is unknown or beside the point. The event noun is formation when the process matters and a structured form when the result does.

semantic_domains.activity: bringing people, parts, or material into an organised arrangement
semantic_domains.creation: a definite structure coming into existence
semantic_domains.physical: matter acquiring an observable arrangement or boundary
